### Post by tytons on 2008-05-25

i couldnt not change my desktop effects as well.its stuck to none.

could any 1 assist me?

here wats in my xorg.conf file now

```
# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using

#

# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver		"synaptics"
	Option		"SendCoreEvents"	"true"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/psaux"
	Option		"Protocol"		"auto-dev"
	Option		"HorizEdgeScroll"	"0"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Configured Video Device"
	Option		"UseFBDev"		"true"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Configured Monitor"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Monitor		"Configured Monitor"
	Device		"Configured Video Device"
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection
```

heres the outcome of the script

```
Checking if it's possible to run Compiz on your system...

 blacklisted in Ubuntu "Hardy Heron".

 In case you already used Compiz successfully on Ubuntu 7.10 (Gutsy), it is
 safe to skip the blacklist. 

Do you want to skip blacklist checks by Compiz? (y/N) y
benjamin@benjamin-laptop:~/Desktop$ ./compiz-check
```

how come the 2nd time i run the script it shows ok for all?and if its the graphic card that doesnt support compiz..how come it ran ok for the 1st time?hmm

---

### Post by Forlong on 2008-05-26
> **tytons said:**
> how come the 2nd time i run the script it shows ok for all?
Because you chose to skip the blacklist.

You should be able to run Compiz now.
> **tytons said:**
> and if its the graphic card that doesnt support compiz..how come it ran ok for the 1st time?hmm
Please read what "I" said in the output.
The combination **laptop + ATI-card + radeon driver** is blacklisted on hardy, because of a bug in the driver. It didn't say anything at all about your graphics card not being able to run Compiz.

---

### Post by tytons on 2008-05-26
ah yes..it works now..so wat do i do if i face the same prob again?

hmm its also very laggy now with compiz running..any idea why?

---

### Post by Forlong on 2008-05-26
> **tytons said:**
> ah yes..it works now..so wat do i do if i face the same prob again?
You won't, because Compiz-Check "told" the /usr/bin/compiz script to ignore the blacklist.
This has been stored in a config file of your home directory.

If you choose to create a new user (or delete your home directory or whatever) and you are unable to run Compiz, simply run Compiz-Check again. It will take care of that for you.
> **tytons said:**
> hmm its also very laggy now with compiz running..any idea why?
No, I'm the get-things-run kinda guy. Optimization is not my métier.

You might want to start a new thread for that or search the forums for your graphics card.

---

### Post by tytons on 2008-05-26
alright.

thanks for the wonderful script btw...\\:D/

### Post by Forlong on 2008-05-28
First of all: you shouldn't install any drivers yourself if you don't know 100% what you're doing.
Always use *System &#8594; Administration &#8594; Hardware Drivers*

Second of all, change this:
```
Section "Device"
	...
	Driver		"nv"
	...
EndSection
```
to
```
Section "Device"
	...
	Driver		"nvidia"
	...
EndSection
```

---
